chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Obligatory Upon One Who Approaches a Sleeping Man, and When He Turns on His Back, He Awakens and Kills Him

parts.text: أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ اَلْأَوَّلِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ
هُوَ رَاقِدٌ فَلَمَّا صَارَ عَلَى ظَهْرِهِ اِنْتَبَهَ فَبَعَجَهُ بَعْجَةً فَقَتَلَهُ

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5360 - Al-Husayn ibn Khalid narrated from Abu Al-Hasan Al-Awwal (as) that he was asked about a man who approached another man while he was lying down. When he got on top of him, the man woke up and struck him with a severe blow, killing him.
Imam (as) said: "There is neither blood money (compensation) for him nor retaliation (legal retribution)."
